title: empetrum nigrum subsp. subholarcticum
intro: Empetrum nigrum subsp. subholarcticum, also known as the subholarctic crowberry, is a subspecies of the crowberry plant, a type of low-growing shrub that belongs to the heath family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Empetrum nigrum subsp. subholarcticum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and its berries can be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
